title: Transactor Self-Healing: Phase 3 — Client Convergence
description: Refactor MCP HulyClient to use internal login flow matching REST. Refactor REST initialization to trust selected internal endpoint or router. Remove raw transactor list from default runtime path. See TRANSACTOR_SELF_HEALING_PRD.md Phase 3.

title: Refactor MCP HulyClient to use internal login flow
description: Replace MCP HulyClient._attemptConnection() (HulyClient.js L129) with the REST-style internal handshake: login → selectWorkspace('internal') → use returned token + canonical endpoint. Remove the public URL rewrite dependence from MCP. Both REST and MCP must use identical session establishment.

title: Refactor REST initialization to trust internal endpoint
description:
Refactor REST initialization so the canonical runtime target comes from the internal endpoint model, not from an operator-maintained raw node list.

Behavior by phase:
- if Phase 2 router exists, REST should prefer the router endpoint
- otherwise, REST should trust the workspace-selected internal endpoint returned by `selectWorkspace(..., 'internal')`
- raw `config.transactorUrls` must not override either of the above in the steady-state path

This bead is about choosing the right source of truth for the primary connection target inside REST startup and recovery logic.
acceptance_criteria: REST startup and reconnection logic derive the primary runtime target from the internal endpoint model; raw transactor lists no longer override the selected internal endpoint or router; startup logs make the chosen canonical endpoint explicit.

title: Remove raw transactor list from default runtime path
description: Ensure HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS / equivalent raw node lists are not consumed at runtime by REST or MCP for connection decisions. They may remain as fallback config or documentation but must not override the canonical endpoint selection flow.

title: Transactor Self-Healing: Phase 2 — Canonical Router
description: Add a transactor-router service backed only by auth-healthy members. Point account/workspace/REST/MCP to ws://transactor-router:3333. See TRANSACTOR_SELF_HEALING_PRD.md Phase 2.

title: Add transactor-router service to docker-compose
description:
Add a durable internal `transactor-router` service to `docker-compose.yml` as the single WebSocket entrypoint for internal Huly traffic.

Requirements:
- expose `ws://transactor-router:3333`
- support WebSocket upgrade correctly
- route only to members that have passed authenticated health admission
- avoid generic blind round-robin across raw transactor nodes
- be implementable with a lightweight proxy or minimal custom service

Design note:
The router must be introduced as an internal stability boundary. REST, MCP, account, and workspace should no longer need to know individual transactor instance addresses once this exists.

Out of scope:
- client convergence work inside MCP/REST
- probe implementation details beyond what the router needs for membership
acceptance_criteria: A transactor-router service exists in docker-compose; internal clients can open WebSocket sessions through ws://transactor-router:3333; router does not forward to nodes that have failed authenticated admission; service passes an end-to-end internal WS validation.

title: Implement auth-healthy membership management for router
description: Router must only forward to transactors that pass the authenticated health probe. Implement membership add/remove: transactors enter pool only after probe passes, leave pool on consecutive failures. Avoid routing to partially corrupted nodes.

title: Point account and workspace services to transactor-router
description:
Update the core Huly services that currently publish a direct internal transactor address so they instead advertise `ws://transactor-router:3333`.

This bead is specifically about the env vars that define the canonical internal transactor endpoint for the platform, not generic account URLs.

Primary targets:
- `docker-compose.yml` account service `TRANSACTOR_URL`
- `docker-compose.yml` workspace service `TRANSACTOR_URL`
- any adjacent core service config that still embeds a raw `transactor-N` address as the internal WS endpoint

Goal:
After this change, the platform-advertised internal endpoint and the actual runtime endpoint model are the same.
acceptance_criteria: Account and workspace services advertise ws://transactor-router:3333 as the internal transactor endpoint via TRANSACTOR_URL or equivalent canonical config; no core service continues to publish raw transactor-N addresses as the internal WS target.

title: Point REST and MCP to transactor-router
description: Update REST API and MCP server configuration to connect through ws://transactor-router:3333 instead of direct transactor endpoints. This replaces the Phase 0 single-node pin with the durable routed endpoint.

title: Transactor Self-Healing: Phase 4 — Route Resilience
description: Harden REST and MCP route-level resilience. Change /api/issues/:id to use pool selection. Change /health to run authenticated checks. Move project issue counts off request path. See TRANSACTOR_SELF_HEALING_PRD.md Phase 4.

title: Change /api/issues/:id to use pool selection
description: Currently /api/issues/:identifier uses hulyClient directly (server.js L2275), which pins to a potentially stale primary. Change to use healthy pooled client selection so single-issue reads fail fast or succeed from a healthy client, never hang on a dead primary.

title: Change /health to run authenticated checks
description:
Update `/health` and `/health/detailed` so they report real service truth, not just object existence.

Requirements:
- do not treat `hulyClient !== null` as sufficient for healthy status
- run a cheap authenticated query through the same connection model users depend on
- if the primary path is stale but a healthy pooled path exists, surface degraded vs healthy accurately
- include enough structured detail to distinguish: no client, stale primary, reduced pool capacity, and authenticated query failure

This bead should make health endpoints useful for operators and for automated recovery logic.
acceptance_criteria: Health endpoints fail or degrade based on authenticated query truth instead of client object presence; responses distinguish stale primary from healthy pooled fallback; operators can tell from /health output whether the service is merely alive or actually usable.

title: Move project issue counts off request path
description:
Remove synchronous global issue counting from the `/api/projects` request path.

Preferred implementation order:
1. serve counts from cache or precomputed metadata
2. refresh counts asynchronously on a bounded interval or event-driven trigger
3. only fall back to expensive recomputation in non-user-facing maintenance flows

The fix must preserve useful project list output without forcing a full issue scan on every request.
acceptance_criteria: /api/projects returns without a synchronous full issue scan; project counts come from cache or precomputed state; endpoint remains responsive under degraded transactor capacity and no longer trips the existing 15s timeout during normal reads.

title: Add per-route timeout and fallback counters
description: Expose observability metrics per FR-4: per-route timeout counts, fallback-to-pool counts, query duration histograms. No route may wait longer than its configured timeout without returning a structured error. Add counters for monitoring and alerting.

title: Investigate transactor auth probe flapping across nodes
description: Transactor-1 consistently fails the authenticated health probe (websocket stage timeout ~5s) while transactors 2-5 pass. This persists across clean restarts where startup logs show no errors (load-table succeeds, Kafka joins). The SDK connect() socketFactory fires but the protocol handshake never completes. REST /health/detailed also times out when pinned to transactor-1, confirming the failure is real. Repinned Phase 0 to transactor-4 as workaround. Root cause may be: corrupted workspace state in CockroachDB specific to transactor-1's consumer group, or a Kafka partition assignment issue.
notes:
2026-03-14 findings:
- The earlier transactor-1-specific framing is stale; auth probe failures now flap between nodes.
- Rebuilt huly-rest-api and huly-mcp images after fixing Dockerfile.v07 symlink generation to use package.json names and replace existing links explicitly. This corrected stale links such as @hcengineering/client -> /huly/plugins/client and @hcengineering/core -> /huly/models/core.
- Live SDK probe results remain non-deterministic across nodes, but current direct REST-side createClient succeeds to ws://transactor-1:3333 (~579ms).
- Latest transactor logs show the deeper cause: live Cockroach connectivity failures during session-init windows. Examples: CONNECT_TIMEOUT, CONNECTION_CLOSED, and CONNECTION_ENDED to cockroachdb:26257.
- Cockroach logs show a restart at 2026-03-14T04:47:54Z after disk stall / unable to sync log files.
- Current live mitigation: REST/MCP repinned to ws://transactor-1:3333. /health is green again. /health/detailed still times out and remains a separate REST route issue.
2026-03-14 watchdog deployment:
- Added huly-watchdog sidecar in docker-compose.yml. It runs scripts/huly-watchdog.sh every 60s with AUTO_RECOVER=true.
- Watchdog is now container-safe: it uses Docker labels instead of docker compose, probes REST/MCP via service URLs, and restarts at most one transactor per cycle with a 300s cooldown.
- Recovery is gated on Cockroach health; if Cockroach is unhealthy, transactor restarts are skipped instead of thrashing the whole pool.
- First live cycle completed successfully: transactor-1 had 1/2 auth probe failures, other transactors passed, REST and MCP were healthy, and no restart was triggered yet.

title: Transactor Self-Healing: Phase 0 — Emergency Stabilization
description: Pin REST and MCP to a single known-good internal transactor. Remove raw HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS from the production traffic path. Keep multi-node topology out of client config until health-based routing exists. See TRANSACTOR_SELF_HEALING_PRD.md Phase 0.

title: Pin REST API to single canonical internal transactor
description:
Phase 0 must be config-first, not an architectural rewrite.

Update the REST API runtime so production traffic goes through exactly one canonical internal transactor endpoint during stabilization. This can be achieved by pinning `HULY_TRANSACTOR_URL` to the chosen node or by honoring the workspace-selected internal endpoint, but Phase 0 must not preserve raw multi-node runtime fanout.

Scope:
- stop using `HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS` for live REST routing
- keep connection behavior simple and deterministic for stabilization
- leave the durable router design to Phase 2

Code anchors:
- `huly-rest-api/server.js` around `selectWorkspace(..., 'internal')` and pool initialization
- `docker-compose.yml` REST API environment section

Out of scope for this bead:
- adding the permanent `transactor-router`
- rewriting all REST route behavior
- full MCP/REST connection convergence
acceptance_criteria: REST API uses exactly one canonical internal transactor endpoint in production; HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS no longer affects live REST routing; /health, /api/projects, and /api/issues/:identifier all respond through the pinned endpoint in validation.

title: Pin MCP server to single canonical internal transactor
description:
Phase 0 MCP stabilization should also be config-first.

Update the MCP server so it stops enumerating raw transactor nodes during emergency stabilization and instead uses one canonical internal transactor endpoint. The goal in this phase is to remove topology ambiguity and stop recovery thrash, not to complete the final connection-model refactor.

Scope:
- pin MCP traffic to one canonical internal endpoint
- ensure `HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS` does not drive live MCP routing during Phase 0
- keep the existing MCP connection implementation otherwise as intact as possible

Code anchors:
- `huly-mcp-server/src/core/HulyClientPool.js`
- `huly-mcp-server/src/core/HulyClient.js`
- `docker-compose.yml` MCP environment section

Out of scope for this bead:
- replacing MCP connect with the REST-style internal login flow
- adding the permanent router
- deep refactors to pool semantics beyond stabilization
acceptance_criteria: MCP server routes production traffic through exactly one canonical internal transactor endpoint; pool initialization no longer fans out across raw node URLs during Phase 0; MCP tool calls succeed without reconnection thrash across dead transactors.

title: Remove raw HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS from production traffic path
description: Remove or neutralize the HULY_TRANSACTOR_URLS environment variable from docker-compose.yml for REST API (L734) and MCP server (L779). These raw transactor lists bypass account-service endpoint selection and create topology disagreement between core Huly services and custom clients. The env var can remain defined but must not be used for runtime connection decisions.

title: Verify docker-compose internal transactor endpoint consistency
description: Audit docker-compose.yml to ensure account service (L500), workspace service (L527), REST API, and MCP server all reference the same canonical internal transactor endpoint. Currently core Huly services and custom clients disagree on internal transactor topology. After this change, all services must converge on one endpoint model.

title: Validate Phase 0 stabilization end-to-end
description: After pinning REST and MCP to canonical endpoint: 1) Verify REST /health returns 200. 2) Verify REST /api/projects returns data. 3) Verify REST /api/issues/:id returns single issue. 4) Verify MCP server connects and serves tool calls. 5) Verify docker compose ps shows all services healthy. 6) Confirm no service references raw transactor node lists at runtime.

title: Transactor Self-Healing: Phase 1 — Authenticated Watchdog
description: Replace transactor port-level healthcheck with an authenticated probe that verifies login, workspace selection, WebSocket connect, and getHierarchy(). Add watchdog restart behavior on consecutive failures. See TRANSACTOR_SELF_HEALING_PRD.md Phase 1.

title: Create transactor-auth-healthcheck.js script
description: Create scripts/transactor-auth-healthcheck.js that: 1) Authenticates using configured Huly credentials. 2) Calls selectWorkspace(workspace, 'internal'). 3) Opens WebSocket session against candidate transactor. 4) Verifies getHierarchy() succeeds. 5) Exits 0 on success, non-zero on failure. Must complete in under 5 seconds under normal conditions.
notes: Implemented scripts/transactor-auth-healthcheck.js with SDK-based login/selectWorkspace/createClient flow. Added node:test coverage in scripts/transactor-auth-healthcheck.test.js. Verified live in huly-huly-mcp:latest on huly-test_default: transactor-4 passes, transactor-1 fails fast with exit 1.

title: Replace Docker healthcheck with authenticated probe
description: Replace the curl-based healthcheck in docker-compose.yml (L211) for all transactors with the authenticated probe script. Configure interval, timeout, retries, and start_period appropriately. Current healthcheck only proves port 3333 is listening — does not detect the partial corruption failure mode.
notes: Replaced the curl-based transactor Docker healthcheck with the authenticated probe script in docker-compose.yml. Mounted scripts/transactor-auth-healthcheck.js into all five transactor containers, added required auth env, and recreated transactors sequentially. Verified all transactors plus huly-rest-api and huly-mcp are healthy after rollout. Important caveat: the in-container raw authenticated path is healthy on transactor-1; the separate external SDK failure remains tracked in HULY-eo9.

title: Add watchdog restart on consecutive probe failures
description: Implement restart-on-consecutive-failure behavior: if a transactor fails N consecutive authenticated probes (recommend N=3), mark it unhealthy for routing and terminate PID 1 inside the container so Docker restarts it. Consider: Docker healthcheck retries may handle this natively, or a sidecar watchdog may be needed.
notes: Implemented authenticated probe counting and restart-on-threshold in scripts/huly-watchdog.sh using the huly-huly-mcp:latest helper image. Verified with AUTO_RECOVER=true and threshold=1: watchdog detected transactor-1 failure, emitted structured probe log, and terminated PID 1 in transactor-1. Important finding: transactor-1 still fails the authenticated websocket probe after restart, while transactor-2/3/4/5 pass.

title: Add structured logging for probe failures
description: Emit structured JSON logs from the health probe on every failure with: transactor node identity, failure stage (login/selectWorkspace/websocket/query), error message, probe duration, consecutive failure count. Enable observability for FR-4 metrics.
notes: Added structured JSON failure logging to scripts/transactor-auth-healthcheck.js with stage, transactor identity, duration, workspace, and consecutive failure count. Added node:test coverage. Verified live in huly-huly-mcp:latest on huly-test_default: transactor-1 emits JSON failure log; transactor-4 succeeds.
